title: Buttondown vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown vs Moosend: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
intro: Both Buttondown and Moosend are freemium email marketing platforms starting at $9/month, but they serve different audiences. Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers who value markdown editing, while Moosend targets small businesses seeking unlimited emails and advanced automation. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right tool for your newsletter or marketing campaigns.
choose_a_if: You write newsletters in markdown and prefer a developer-friendly interface
You need API access for custom integrations and automation
You prioritize simplicity and focus on newsletter writing over advanced marketing features
choose_b_if: You want to send unlimited emails on the free plan with up to 1,000 subscribers
You need landing pages, subscription forms, and A/B testing capabilities
You're building a small business and want comprehensive email marketing and automation workflows

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. Buttondown excels for technical users who love markdown and API access, while Moosend is superior for small businesses wanting unlimited emails and more marketing features. Moosend's larger free tier (1,000 vs 100 subscribers) gives it an edge for budget-conscious startups.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Moosend?
faq.a: Both start at $9/month for paid plans, making them equally affordable. However, Moosend's free tier supports 10x more subscribers (1,000 vs 100), so it's more cost-effective if you're growing an audience slowly. Moosend also offers unlimited emails even on paid plans, while Buttondown's pricing scales with your subscriber count.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality, making migration straightforward. Buttondown explicitly offers import/export features, and Moosend provides data export capabilities. However, you'll need to reconfigure automations and landing pages manually, as these features don't always transfer automatically between platforms.
